title: Shoot for the Moon Day!
story: Every July 20, people across America celebrate National Moon Day by looking up at the sky and remembering one of humanity's greatest adventures. This special day marks when astronauts first walked on the moon, taking giant leaps for all of us! Today, families visit planetariums, build rocket models, and have moon-watching parties in their backyards. Kids dress up as astronauts, eat "space food" like freeze-dried ice cream, and learn cool facts about our lunar neighbor. Many people share moon photos on social media and dream about future space travel. It's the perfect day to reach for the stars!
activity_question: What do families often do in their backyards on National Moon Day?
follow_up_question: If you could visit the moon, what would you want to explore first?

title: Checkmate Champions Celebrate Chess Day
story: Chess players around the world move their pieces with extra excitement on July 20 for International Chess Day! Schools host tournaments where kids battle with knights, bishops, and queens. Chess clubs organize giant outdoor games with human-sized pieces. Libraries set up boards for beginners to learn the ancient strategy game. Online chess websites see millions of matches played as people celebrate this brainy holiday that honors the world's most popular board game.
activity_question: What do libraries typically set up on International Chess Day?
follow_up_question: What chess piece would you want to be and why?

title: Sweet Treats on Sticks Rule the Day!
story: Every July 20, candy lovers everywhere celebrate National Lollipop Day by enjoying their favorite sweet treats on sticks! People share photos of colorful lollipops on social media, from classic cherry and grape flavors to wild new combinations like pickle or bacon. Candy stores offer special deals, and some families even make homemade lollipops together using molds and sugar syrup. The holiday celebrates one of the world's most popular candies that's been making people smile for over a century. Whether you prefer swirled rainbow pops or simple fruit flavors, this sugary celebration gives everyone a perfect excuse to indulge their sweet tooth!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Lollipop Day?
bonus_fact: The largest lollipop ever made weighed over 7,000 pounds!

title: Crack Open Some Lucky Fun!
story: Every July 20, people across America celebrate National Fortune Cookie Day by cracking open those crispy golden treats to read their fortunes! Restaurants give out extra fortune cookies, families buy boxes to share at dinner, and friends compare their funny predictions. Some people even write their own silly fortunes and slip them into cookies for pranks. The best part? You get to eat the crunchy cookie after reading whether you'll "find happiness in small things" or "beware of purple socks." It's the perfect excuse to order Chinese takeout and see what wisdom awaits inside your cookie!
activity_question: What do people often do with fortune cookies on National Fortune Cookie Day besides eating them?
activity_choices: Compare their funny predictions with friends
activity_answer: Compare their funny predictions with friends
bonus_fact: Fortune cookies were actually invented in California, not China!

title: Blast Off to Space Exploration Day!
story: Rockets roar and astronauts float through space on National Space Exploration Day every July 20! This amazing holiday celebrates humanity's incredible journey to the stars and beyond. People mark this special day by visiting space museums, watching rocket launches online, and gazing up at the night sky through telescopes. Kids build model rockets, read books about astronauts, and dream about walking on Mars someday. Science centers host special space-themed events with planetarium shows and hands-on experiments. Many families watch classic moon landing videos or space movies together. Social media fills with stunning photos from NASA and space agencies around the world. It's the perfect day to remember that we're all space explorers on this amazing planet Earth!
activity_question: What do people commonly use to look at the night sky on National Space Exploration Day?
bonus_fact: The first moon landing happened on July 20, 1969!

title: Friends Day: Celebrating Your Squad!
story: Every July 20, people around the world celebrate Friends Day by showing their buddies just how much they care! Friends text each other funny memes, share old photos together, and post sweet messages on social media with hashtags like #FriendsDay and #BestFriendsForever. Some friends plan special hangouts, have movie marathons, or surprise each other with small gifts. Others simply call up old friends they haven't talked to in a while to catch up and reminisce about good times. It's the perfect excuse to tell your friends they're awesome!
activity_question: What do people commonly share on social media for Friends Day?
activity_choices: Pictures of their pets wearing hats
follow_up_question: What's the most creative way you could surprise your best friend on Friends Day?
